title: Define partially legible mapping rules
overview: Create src/visual/mappings.ts with pure functions that translate visitor signals and audio data into visual parameters. Each mapping is indirect — the viewer can feel its influence but never sees raw identifiers. The module exports a VisualParams interface and a computeMappings() function consumed by the render loop.

steps.title: Define VisualParams interface and palette lookup table
steps.description: Create src/visual/mappings.ts with a VisualParams interface that captures all visual parameters the render loop will consume: paletteHue (number 0–360), cadence (number, modulation speed factor), density (number, particle/structure count factor), motionScale (number, amplitude multiplier), fieldDisturbance (number 0–1), bassMacro (number 0–1), trebleShimmer (number 0–1). Define a PALETTE_FAMILIES lookup that maps coarse geo regions to hue ranges using a small hash of country+region strings — never exposing raw values.

steps.title: Implement geo-to-palette mapping
steps.description: Write mapGeoPalette(geo: GeoHint, seed: string): number that hashes country+region into a palette hue (0–360). When geo is null, fall back to seed-derived hue. The mapping is coarse: many countries share the same hue band, making it impossible to reverse-engineer location from colour alone. Use the existing fnv1a-style hash from sessionSeed or a local equivalent to keep it deterministic.

steps.title: Implement timezone/time-to-cadence mapping
steps.description: Write mapTimeCadence(timezone: string, seed: string): number that derives a modulation speed factor (0.5–2.0) from the visitor's timezone offset and current hour. Morning/evening hours produce slower cadence; midday and late night produce faster cadence. The timezone string is hashed — never displayed. The seed adds per-session variation so the same timezone doesn't always produce identical cadence.

steps.title: Implement DPR/capability-to-density mapping
steps.description: Write mapCapabilityDensity(signals: BrowserSignals): number that maps devicePixelRatio and hardwareConcurrency to a density factor (0.3–1.0). High DPR + many cores → higher density; low capability → sparser scene. Null values fall back to mid-range defaults. This provides graceful mobile degradation without exposing exact hardware specs.

steps.title: Implement reduced-motion and pointer mappings
steps.description: Write mapMotionScale(prefersReducedMotion: boolean | null): number returning 0.3 for reduced-motion, 1.0 otherwise. Write mapPointerDisturbance(pointer: PointerState): number that converts pointer speed into a 0–1 field disturbance value with smoothing (not a direct 1:1 map). When pointer is inactive, disturbance decays toward 0.

steps.title: Implement audio-to-visual mappings (bass/treble)
steps.description: Write mapAudioBands(pipeline: AnalyserPipeline): { bassMacro: number; trebleShimmer: number } that averages low-frequency bins (0–~500Hz, roughly bins 0–23 with 2048 FFT at 44.1kHz) for bassMacro and high-frequency bins (~2kHz–8kHz, roughly bins 93–372) for trebleShimmer. Both normalised 0–1. When pipeline data is all zeros (no audio), both return 0.

steps.title: Compose computeMappings() orchestrator function
steps.description: Write computeMappings(signals, geo, seed, pointer, pipeline): VisualParams that calls all individual mapping functions and returns a single VisualParams object. This is the public API the render loop will call each frame. Signal-derived values (palette, cadence, density, motionScale) are computed once at init and cached; per-frame values (fieldDisturbance, bassMacro, trebleShimmer) are computed fresh. Export both the orchestrator and a computeStaticMappings() for init-time values.

steps.title: Write comprehensive tests
steps.description: Create tests/visual/mappings.test.ts with tests covering: (1) geo palette returns consistent hue for same inputs, (2) null geo falls back gracefully, (3) timezone cadence stays in 0.5–2.0 range, (4) capability density clamps to 0.3–1.0, (5) reduced-motion returns lower motionScale, (6) pointer disturbance is 0 when inactive, (7) audio bands return 0 for silent input, (8) no raw identifiers appear in VisualParams output, (9) computeMappings returns all required fields. Target: at least 10 test cases.

steps.title: Verify build and definition of done
steps.description: Run npm run build to confirm no type errors. Run npm run test to confirm all tests pass. Verify no forbidden storage APIs (cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage) are referenced in mappings.ts. Confirm no raw identifiers are exposed in any output.

mapping_summary.method: Hash country+region → hue band (0–360). Many countries share bands. Null → seed-derived fallback.
Timezone offset + current hour → speed factor (0.5–2.0). Seed adds per-session variation.
Clamp and normalise capability indicators → density factor (0.3–1.0). Null → mid-range.
Boolean → 0.3 (reduced) or 1.0 (full). Experience preserved, amplitude lowered.
Pointer speed → smoothed 0–1 disturbance. Decays when inactive. Not a direct coordinate map.
Average FFT bins 0–23 → normalised 0–1. Drives camera drift, field expansion.
Average FFT bins 93–372 → normalised 0–1. Drives point jitter, sparkle.

acceptance_verification.at_least_4_mappings: 7 mappings defined (geo→palette, time→cadence, DPR→density, motion-pref→scale, pointer→disturbance, bass→macro, treble→shimmer)
acceptance_verification.indirect_not_literal: All mappings use hashing or normalisation; no 1:1 correspondence between input and output
acceptance_verification.no_raw_identifiers: VisualParams contains only numeric values; no strings from geo, UA, IP, or fingerprint
acceptance_verification.documented_in_code: Each mapping function has a JSDoc comment explaining the rule; VisualParams interface documents each field's range and purpose

risks: FFT bin ranges for bass/treble depend on sample rate (assumed 44.1kHz); if sample rate differs, bands shift slightly — acceptable for artistic use
Pointer disturbance smoothing needs tuning in practice; initial values are a starting point
